exemplar.title: Comment pouvons-nous faire la distinction entre connaissance, croyance et opinion? (How can we distinguish between knowledge, belief and opinion?)

detailedExemplarData.title: Comment pouvons-nous faire la distinction entre connaissance, croyance et opinion? (How can we distinguish between knowledge, belief and opinion?)

strengths: Clear identification of three specific objects with brief real-world contexts
Basic links established between each object and the selected IA prompt
Some use of evidence and citations to support object justifications
weaknesses: Explanations of how each object illuminates the TOK prompt remain largely declarative and unconvincing
Justifications are superficial, repetitive and lean towards description rather than critical TOK analysis
Heavy reliance on general sources (e.g. Wikipedia) and inconsistent citation formatting
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The official total of 3 places this exhibition firmly in the lower end of the 3â4 band. The student fulfils the requirement to present three specific objects and briefly situate them in real-world contexts, offering elementary links to the TOK prompt and supplying some supporting citations. However, these links are asserted rather than critically examined, and the analysis does not progress beyond descriptive summaries into substantive discussion of ways of knowing, counterclaims or the significance of distinguishing knowledge, belief and opinion. The evidence provided is often generic and tangential, and citation practice is uneven. While the submission meets basic band descriptors, its uneven analytical depth and repetitive justifications justify a final holistic score of 3 out of 10 for the TOK Exhibition criterion.

annotations.comment: The introduction provides context for each object but remains largely descriptive. To deepen the TOK analysis, the student should integrate specific ways of knowing and explore how they influence the categorization of opinion, knowledge and belief.
The exhibition question is presented in French even though the rest of the text is in English. The student should ensure consistency by restating the IA prompt in the exhibition language.

The real-world context for the Olympus camera is simply that it âbelongs to my father.â To strengthen justification, describe how the personal ownership influences its role in distinguishing opinion.
There is no explicit reference to the selected IA prompt in this section. The student should quote or paraphrase the prompt directly to anchor the link between the object and the TOK question.
The label âObjet 1 â Appareil Photo Olympus OM-1â is generic. The student could enrich the caption by indicating why this specific photograph exemplifies opinion.

The description of how the camera transmits perspective is illustrative but still descriptive. The student should examine how the choice of focal length or framing affects the subjectivity of opinion.
The sculpture header introduces a new object but does not connect it back to the IA prompt. The student should explicitly state how this object illuminates the prompt about distinguishing knowledge.
The physical description of the sculpture is thorough but remains at the level of observation. The student should analyze how the dynamic equilibrium demonstrates an epistemic process in physics.
The definition of knowledge echoes a dictionary entry and is tautological. The student should engage with TOK concepts (e.g., justification, certainty) to deepen the analysis.

The assertion that the sculpture âembodies the law of conservation of energyâ is declarative and lacks critical examination. The student could discuss counterclaims, such as energy losses due to friction.
The label for the kinetic sculpture object is purely descriptive. The student should clarify how the sculptureâs movement captures the transition from belief to knowledge.
The Akuaba context description is rich but remains anthropological. The student should link the ritual practice directly to how belief shapes human understanding in TOK terms.
The belief definition is cursory and mirrors a glossary. The student should explore how belief interacts with emotion and faith as ways of knowing.
The comparison to symbols like the cross or Buddha is generic. The student should critically evaluate how different belief symbols operate within distinct areas of knowledge.
The label âObjet 3 â PoupÃ©e africaine Â« Akuaba Â»â is functional but lacks TOK context. The student should illustrate how this object externalizes internal belief processes.
The header âPoup  africaine  Akuabaâ contains spacing errors. Ensure accurate spelling and formatting for clarity.
The conclusion restates the objects but does not reflect on the implications for distinguishing knowledge, belief and opinion. A stronger synthesis would revisit challenges and counterclaims.
There is no discussion of why distinguishing these terms matters in real-world contexts. The exhibition would benefit from an explicit evaluation of its broader significance.
The reference list relies heavily on Wikipedia, which is not a scholarly source. Incorporate peer-reviewed articles or TOK textbooks to strengthen evidence.
Some URLs are excessively long and not formatted according to a citation style. Use a consistent format (e.g., MLA, APA) and include DOIs where available.
The reference entries vary in date formatting and completeness. Ensure uniform citation elements: author, year, title, source, URL or DOI.
Consider adding annotations in the reference list to highlight how each source supports specific TOK claims within the text.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
